query: How active is the 2026 US tornado season and what is the forecast/outlook for early July 2026 severe weather? Current ENSO state (La Nina/El Nino) for summer 2026 and any Climate Prediction Center outlook for July 2026 severe convective weather in northern Plains and Midwest.
answer:
I've completed the research and delivered the structured findings. Here's a summary of the key findings:

**2026 US Tornado Season** — Highly variable and running roughly average-to-below-recent-years. ~1,100–1,141 preliminary tornado reports and 78 deaths through late June (vs. 1,811 in 2024, 1,559 in 2025). Notable pattern: below-average January/May, but extremely active March (204) and June (304 — most active June since 2010). Illinois has surprisingly led the nation, and two EF5s occurred (Indiana in March, first-ever Minnesota EF5 in May).

**Early July 2026 severe weather** — An active northern Plains/Upper Midwest corridor. SPC issued ENHANCED risks July 1, 2, and 4, and a SLIGHT RISK on July 7 (northern High Plains into Upper Midwest) with large hail and damaging winds primary, isolated tornado potential — a classic summer MCS "ring of fire" pattern. Extreme heat dominates the mid-month CPC hazards outlook.

**Summer 2026 ENSO** — An **El Niño Advisory is in effect** (not La Niña). El Niño is present and strengthening: Niño-3.4 at +0.7°C (CPC, June 11), rising to +1.7°C weekly per IRI, with a 63% chance of very strong El Niño by Nov–Jan. The La Niña→El Niño transition was cited as suppressing spring severe weather.

**CPC July 2026 outlook (N. Plains/Midwest)** — Equal Chances for temperature and a weak tilt toward below-normal precipitation. CPC doesn't issue tornado-specific monthly outlooks (that's SPC's Day 1–8 domain).

One data caveat I flagged: the iweathernet source appeared to be a stale/partial cache; I relied on the more current national totals from SPC-record-based trackers and NWS monthly compilations.

sources.title: CPC ENSO Diagnostic Discussion
sources.snippet: El Niño conditions developed; latest weekly Niño-3.4 index +0.7°C. El Niño Advisory in effect (issued June 11, 2026); 63% chance of very strong El Niño Nov-Jan.

sources.title: IRI ENSO Forecast (June 2026 Quick Look)
sources.snippet: El Niño strengthening; Niño-3.4 anomaly +0.94°C in May 2026, latest weekly value +1.7°C (June 17). Published June 22, 2026.

sources.title: SPC Day 1 Convective Outlook — July 7, 2026
sources.snippet: Slight Risk from northern High Plains into Upper Midwest; large hail and severe wind gusts possible, isolated tornado potential.

sources.title: SPC Jul 1, 2026 Day 1 Convective Outlook (Enhanced Risk Midwest/Upper Great Lakes)
sources.snippet: Enhanced risk across Midwest into Upper Great Lakes; damaging wind gusts to 80 mph.

sources.title: CPC 30-Day Outlook Discussion for July 2026
sources.snippet: Equal Chances temperature across eastern northern/central Plains to Upper Midwest; weak tilt toward below-normal precipitation over north-central CONUS. Issued June 30, 2026.

sources.title: CPC Probabilistic Hazards Outlook (valid July 14-20, 2026)
sources.snippet: Amplified mid-level high over Four Corners brings extreme heat risk to much of western/central/southeastern CONUS; enhanced monsoon and heavy precip risk.

sources.title: Tornadoes of 2026: Season Tracker, 78 Deaths, Illinois Leads
sources.snippet: More than 1,100 preliminary tornado reports through late June, 78 dead, first Minnesota EF5; May 19-26 stretch produced 162 tornadoes in 24 hours.

sources.title: List of United States tornadoes from May to June 2026
sources.snippet: May well below average (119 confirmed); June very active (304 confirmed) — most active June since 2010; La Niña to El Niño transition suppressed May.

sources.title: List of United States tornadoes from January to March 2026
sources.snippet: January 21 (below avg), February 49 (above avg), March 204 confirmed — 3rd most active March on record.

sources.title: America's top tornado state in 2026 is surprising (Illinois)
sources.snippet: Through June 22, 196 combined reports/confirmed tornadoes in Illinois — far ahead of any other state.

sources.title: How many Tornadoes in the US in 2026? (Polymarket)
sources.snippet: Market resolving on NCEI final 2026 tornado counts; reflects uncertainty on full-year total.

data_points.metric: US preliminary tornado reports YTD (through late June 2026)
CPC chance of very strong El Niño during Nov-Jan 2026-27
MLCAPE along surface front (SD/MN) July 7 outlook
